source_description: A miraging clipper delay. From a clipper- quickly plant the setting leg and bring the support leg over the bag from in to out and in one motion- keeping this leg close to the other leg- into a clipper delay on the other side. Or, setting from left toe, quickly plant the left leg and bring the right leg up and around the bag from in to out. Turn slightly in the direction of the dexterity (in this case, to the right) to help bring the right foot back around into the cross body position to end the move on a right foot clipper delay.
parser_notes: pt11 ruled drifter = miraging clipper = 3 ADD. Branch family-anchor.

source_description: From either a clipper or a toe delay, set the footbag up- then with the support leg circle the footbag from the front up and over the footbag. Hop off of the original setting leg as you finish the dexterity and catch the bag on a clipper delay. It is easiest to point your toes down as you circle the bag because this makes it faster and less likely to snag the bag as you circle it.

provenance_notes: FB.org parenthetical "(Quantum Mirage)" + Red pt2 ruling agree. | FB.org 3-ADD confirms 'Toe Blur' (source_adds=3; jobs_notation_raw='TOE > OP IN [DEX] > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]'; component_flags='[dex] [dex] [del]'); ingested 2026-05-21.

source_description: Circle the footbag in between toe kicks.
parser_notes: 1-add kick-only motion; no closing stall in this row. Sibling 2-add Around the World (TOE > SAME IN/OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]) is the canonical compound.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP [XBD]
source_description: Kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the support leg.
parser_notes: Kick form; no closing stall. Sibling 2-add Clipper (CLIP [XBD] [DEL]) is the canonical compound.
equivalent_to: clipper-stall (live DB; with stall finish at 2 ADD)

symbolic_notation_raw: CALF [UNS]
source_description: Kick the footbag with the calf of the leg.
parser_notes: Unusual-surface kick on calf. Cloud Delay (2-add) is the stall counterpart.

symbolic_notation_raw: flying>inside
source_description: Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot.
parser_notes: Per curator adjudication 2026-05-21: flying(1) → 1 ADD; promoted to first-class.
provenance_notes: curator interpretation 2026-05-21

symbolic_notation_raw: flying>outside
source_description: Jump and kick the footbag with the outside surface of the foot.
parser_notes: Per curator adjudication 2026-05-21: flying(1) → 1 ADD; promoted to first-class.
provenance_notes: curator interpretation 2026-05-21

symbolic_notation_raw: FOREHEAD [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the forehead.
parser_notes: Specialized surface stall.

add_formula: dex(1) + stall(1) --> 1 ADD (folk reduced; canonical decomposition deferred)
source_description: Frigidosis is an inside delay caught behind your body, but not in the cross-body position. The best way to visualize it is to go for an osis, make the catch, but don't spin. So, you "freeze" the bag in a non-cross-body position.
parser_notes: pt11-deferred per project_freestyle_state memory (Frigidosis decomposition Red to look into; not yet settled).
unresolved_questions: Canonical Jobs notation; whether the "freeze without spin" qualifier counts as a distinct atom or a positional cue on inside-delay.
provenance_notes: pt11 Red consultation deferred this trick

symbolic_notation_raw: HEAD [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the top of the head. Look slightly upwards to the get the top of the head perfectly flat.
parser_notes: Specialized surface stall; pt8 ruled Head/Peak/Shoulder Stalls = 1 ADD each.

symbolic_notation_raw: INSIDE [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the inside surface of the foot.
parser_notes: Foundational stall surface. Asterisk in source indicates video demonstration available.

symbolic_notation_raw: KNEE [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the top of the thigh.
parser_notes: Foundational stall surface.

symbolic_notation_raw: NECK [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the back of the neck.
parser_notes: Multiple neck-stall variants exist by entry side and head tilt; this is the baseline atom.

symbolic_notation_raw: OUTSIDE [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the outside surface of the foot.
parser_notes: Foundational stall surface. Asterisk indicates video demonstration available.

symbolic_notation_raw: PEAK [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the peak of your cap/hat. This can be dismounted by a flick of the head or 'pinging' your peak.
parser_notes: Cap-peak surface stall; pt8 ruled = 1 ADD.

symbolic_notation_raw: SHOULDER [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the shoulder. Use your cheek at first to help to catch the footbag.
parser_notes: Specialized surface stall; pt8 ruled Head/Peak/Shoulder Stalls = 1 ADD each.

symbolic_notation_raw: SOLE [UNS]
source_description: Kick the footbag with the sole of the foot.
parser_notes: Per curator adjudication 2026-05-21: unusual surface(1) → 1 ADD; promoted to first-class. Kick form (no [DEL]); Sole Delay (2-add) is the stall counterpart.
provenance_notes: curator interpretation 2026-05-21

symbolic_notation_raw: [BOD]
source_description: Spin around in between kicks/delays.
parser_notes: Foundational body-rotation atom. Distinct from spin-flag in osis decomposition and from the spinning body modifier per [[project_freestyle_state]] disambiguation.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the top of the shoe (above the toes).
parser_notes: Foundational stall surface; the default surface for sets and many stalls. Asterisk indicates video demonstration available.

symbolic_notation_raw: double knee
source_description: Single flying-body movement; missing from fborg-1add.txt ingest. Added manually as observational intake row.
parser_notes: Per curator adjudication 2026-05-21: flying(1) → 1 ADD; promoted to first-class. Single flying-body movement (not a compound).
provenance_notes: manual curator addition 2026-05-21 (not in fborg-1add.txt)

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN/OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: Circle the footbag in between toe delays. Either in to out or out to in. (Both directions are worth the same amount of adds).
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source. Reverse direction (positional reverse, +0) reaches the same 2 ADD.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the inside surface of the foot with the catching leg tucked behind the support leg. Bend the support leg deeply and lean slightly over this leg for balance.
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom. Asterisk indicates video demonstration available.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > OP OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: Clipper set Illusion. 2 adds.
parser_notes: Folk name (Bubba) with explicit Jobs notation in source. Reads as a clipper-set illusion variant.
provenance_notes: source explicitly notes parenthetical alt-name: "Clipper set Illusion"

symbolic_notation_raw: CALF [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the calf of the leg.
parser_notes: Foundational stall variant on calf surface. Pairs with 1-add Cloud Kick.

symbolic_notation_raw: [BOD] [BOD]
source_description: Do two complete 360 degree spins inbetween kicks.
parser_notes: Multiplicity compound (two spins). Per pt8 multiplicity rule and project_freestyle_state, double-spin was reclassified as a modifier 2026-05-19. Treated here as observational intake.
unresolved_questions: Is double-spin a canonical 2-ADD trick or strictly a modifier on body-spin? Live DB classification shifted; spreadsheet intake preserves both possibilities.

source_description: A cross body outside delay.
parser_notes: Folk-named cross-body outside delay. Source description is brief; specialized variant.
unresolved_questions: Is Dragon a distinct canonical trick or an alias for a cross-body outside stall variant?

source_description: Pass a leg over the footbag from in to out then jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the support leg. E.g., bring the left leg over the footbag from in to out. Now jump off the right leg and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the right foot and land on this leg.
parser_notes: Per curator adjudication 2026-05-21: flying(1) + dex(1) → 2 ADDs; first_class=false for now (observational/foundation intake only).
unresolved_questions: Whether the dex precedes or follows the flying body action structurally.
provenance_notes: curator interpretation 2026-05-21

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: From a toe delay- circle the footbag from out to in and catch on the toe of the support leg.
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source. Fairy-as-modifier still pt12 Q4-open per project memory.

source_description: A cross body sole kick. Easiest with the knee pointing straight down and hit earlier than other kicks.
parser_notes: pt6 ruled Flapper Stall = 3 ADD; this 2-ADD entry is the kick form. Source treats them as distinct.
unresolved_questions: Distinction between Flapper (kick) and pt6-ruled Flapper Stall (3 ADD).

source_description: Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the jumping leg. It's easiest to land on the same leg that kicks the bag. This move is also called "Jester".
parser_notes: Body-flying variant of Clipper Kick. Sibling alias "Jester" preserved.
unresolved_questions: Canonical naming preference: Flying Clipper vs Jester.

symbolic_notation_raw: HEEL [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the heel of the foot. Point toes at their max to create the easiest stalling surface between the calf and the heel.
parser_notes: Specialized surface stall on heel.

source_description: Hold an inside delay close to the ground and hop over it with the support leg.
parser_notes: Technique-focused 2-ADD body-flying-over-stall trick.
unresolved_questions: Whether "Hop Over" is a canonical trick or an instructional technique alias for a body-over-inside-delay.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: From a toe or a clipper delay, set the bag straight up about waist high. As you plant your set leg, bring your support leg up and over the bag from out to in (a reverse miraging motion). Plant the dexterity leg and catch the bag on the toe of the setting foot.
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source.

source_description: Jump and kick the footbag with the knee tucked behind the kicking leg.
parser_notes: Body-flying clipper variant with knee position.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: Pass the support leg over the footbag and catch on a delay. E.g., from a left toe stall, hop off the right leg and bring it over the top of the footbag and catch it on a right toe delay.
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: From a toe delay or a clipper delay, set the bag straight up, and swing the support leg from in to out over the footbag, catching it with the setting leg on a toe delay. The set from a clipper delay is easier once you know how to do a clipper. Note: with a toe-set mirage, the support leg has to first swing under, then back over the bag!
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source. Family-anchor for the mirage cohort per DP-1 FAMILY_INVARIANTS.

source_description: Delay the footbag on the toes on its way down to the ground and continue the downward motion keeping the leg perfectly straight. Keeping a smooth motion swing your leg back and bend at the knee flicking the footbag over your head to the ground in front of you. The key to the pendulum is smooth acceleration while keeping your leg straight as long as physically possible.
parser_notes: Swing-element compound; descending-arc technique. Sibling Rake is the reverse-direction companion (per Sprint 6 in resolved formulas; FM lists rake at 3 ADD vs IFPA 2).
unresolved_questions: Whether Pendulum + Rake should be canonical or remain folk-named technique entries.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source. Source description was empty in fborg-2add.txt.

symbolic_notation_raw: KNEE-CROOK [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Catch the footbag in the crook of the leg. It is easiest to point the knee straight down and bend the support leg slightly for balance. Wait until the bag hits the back of the knee before squeezing it.
parser_notes: pt8 ruled Squeeze/Pincher = 2 ADDs each. Source parenthetical "(Squeeze)" preserved as alternate name.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: From a toe delay- circle the footbag from in to out and catch on the toe of the support leg.
parser_notes: Canonical 2-ADD foundational atom with explicit Jobs notation in source.

source_description: This is a clipper stall set from the opposite side shoulder. The footbag must pass behind the head- ie. blind.
parser_notes: Specialized clipper-stall variant with blind shoulder-set entry. Historical/observational; not in live DB.
unresolved_questions: Whether Probe is a canonical trick or a folk name for a blind-set clipper variant.

source_description: Opposite of a pendulum. Toe delay the footbag behind you (actually, more to your side), and scoop the footbag, swinging your leg forward and across your front.
parser_notes: Reverse-direction companion to Pendulum (per Sprint 6 resolved formulas). FootbagMoves lists rake at 3 ADD; IFPA treats it as 2 per swing-element doctrine. Red review pending per resolved-formulas provenance.
unresolved_questions: ADD divergence: IFPA 2 vs FM 3.

symbolic_notation_raw: SOLE [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Delay the footbag on the sole of the foot. To ensure a flat level landing surface- it is easiest to point the knee straight down and bend the support leg slightly for balance.
parser_notes: pt6 ruled Sole Stall = 2 ADD (real exception to 1-ADD stall convention). Foundational specialized stall.

source_description: Jump and kick the footbag with the toes with the kicking leg tucked behind the jumping leg.
parser_notes: Body-flying clipper variant with toe contact.

source_description: Put an inside delay on the ground and step over it with the opposite leg.
parser_notes: Ground-stall walk-over technique. Sibling to Hop Over.
unresolved_questions: Whether Walk Over is canonical or instructional.

source_description: Inside delay the footbag and pull the footbag around your support leg into a cross body position. It is easiest to use the instep of your shoe to rest the footbag against as you wrap it around.
parser_notes: Specialized wrap-around technique; not in live DB.
unresolved_questions: Whether Wrap is a canonical trick or an instructional wrap-around mechanic.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > JUMP [BOD] > SAME or OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD]
source_description: A leg over straight to a flying clipper. E.g., from a right toe delay, set the footbag straight up and slightly towards yourself. Hop off the right leg and pass the left leg over the footbag- Hit the footbag with the inside surface of the right foot and then in this case it is easiest to land on the left leg.
parser_notes: Body-flying butterfly variant. No stall terminator. Observational.
unresolved_questions: Whether Butterfly Kick is a canonical trick or a body-flying variant alias.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > FLAP [UNS] [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: A cross body sole delay. It is easiest to get a flat surface if you point you knee straight down.
parser_notes: pt6-ruled Flapper Stall = 3 ADD. Specialized cross-body sole delay.
provenance_notes: source explicitly notes parenthetical alt-name: "Cross-Body Sole Delay"

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: Circle the footbag twice between toe delays. Pointing your toes down will prevent the circling leg from snagging the footbag as you come around for the second time.
parser_notes: pt8 ruled DATW = community-stabilized doubling exception. Alt notation: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] > SAME OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL] (both directions worth same).

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
add_formula: dex(1) + dex(1) + stall(1) --> 3 ADDs (= miraging legover per pt4)
source_description: A leg over straight after a mirage. The mirage can be set either from a clipper or a toe delay. From the set hop off the support leg then the setting leg and pass both over the footbag and finally catch the footbag with the setting foot on a toe delay.
parser_notes: pt4 ruled DLO = miraging legover = 3 ADD. Community-stabilized doubling per pt8.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > OP IN [DEX] > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: Basically an in to out double around the world set from a clipper delay. E.g., from a left clipper delay, set the bag just above waist height. Quickly plant the left leg and circle the footbag from in to out twice landing on right toe delay.
parser_notes: Clipper-set double-dex pickup variant.
unresolved_questions: Whether Double Pickup is a canonical trick or a clipper-set variant of DATW.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: A double dexterity legover. E.g., from a left toe delay, hop off the right leg and circle the footbag from out to in twice ending on a right toe delay.
parser_notes: Double-dex legover variant.
unresolved_questions: Whether Double Switch-Over is canonical or a doubling alias.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
parser_notes: pt6 ruled Reverse Drifter = Grifter (separate canonical, not descriptive). Direction-variant of drifter.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > (jump) [BOD] > SAME or OP INSIDE [DEL] > OP OUT [DEX] > (land)
source_description: A hop-over performed in the air. Jump and imediately initiate an inside delay. Bring the jumping leg over the delaying leg and back down to the ground end in a clipper delay.
parser_notes: pt1-ruled jump-bearing 3-ADD. Historical alias "Catwalk" preserved.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > SAME OUT [PDX[DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: A Paradox Revese Mirage. From a clipper set, set the bag about waist high. Turn toward the bag and complete an out to in dexterity with the setting foot. Complete the move by catching the bag on your opposite toe.
parser_notes: Paradox operator on illusion base. Source notation has typo "[PDX[DEX]" (missing closing bracket); intended "[PDX] [DEX]".

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > SAME IN [PDX] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: E.g., from a right clipper delay, set the bag so it's moving across your body from left to right, then bring your right leg around the left leg and around the footbag as it falls. You must in this case circle the footbag from the left of the footbag to the front and finally to the right ending on a left toe delay. Basically the paradox leg completes a tight sharp 'S' shape with the support leg in the first loop and the footbag in the second loop of the letter 'S'.
parser_notes: Paradox modifier on mirage base. First-class pilot member.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > (no plant while) OP IN [BOD] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: E.g., from a right toe delay- jump hard off the support leg and over the footbag when it's falling down. Scoop the footbag up before it hits the ground with the setting foot which never touches the ground.
parser_notes: Symposium modifier on mirage base. No-plant-while qualifier per pt7 symposium semantics. First-class pilot member.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > (back or front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME or OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: Spin into a clipper delay. It is easiest to set this from a clipper stall on the other foot or from a toe delay with the other foot, straight in front of you (not behind you). E.g., from a right toe set, set straight in front of you, about waist high, and turn to the left so the footbag passes behind your back... Keep your head down with your eyes on the footbag until just before it lands on the inside surface of your right foot. Then turn quickly to the left (counterclockwise) and catch the footbag on a clipper delay.
parser_notes: Foundational rotational atom; spin-flag (atomic) + xbody + stall = 3 ADD. Family-anchor.

symbolic_notation_raw: SET > OP INSIDE [DEL] > OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD]
source_description: Start by inside delaying the footbag. Now while wrapping the footbag complete a 360 degree spin towards the support leg. Eliminate any pauses between the initial inside delay and spin to get the move looking nice and smooth.
parser_notes: pt6 ruled refraction = dex (classification settled).

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > OP IN [DEX] > OP PINCHER [UNS] [DEL]
source_description: Eg. From a right clipper delay- pass the left leg over the footbag and twist your hips enabling your right leg to catch the footbag in a squeeze.
parser_notes: Miraging modifier on pincher base. Alternate name "Stepping op Squeeze" preserved.
unresolved_questions: Whether miraging+pincher or stepping+squeeze is the canonical decomposition framing.

symbolic_notation_raw: [BOD] [BOD] [BOD]
source_description: Complete three 360 degree spins inbetween kicks.
parser_notes: Multiplicity compound (three spins). Like double-spin, may be modifier rather than canonical trick.
unresolved_questions: Whether triple-spin is a canonical 3-ADD trick or strictly a modifier on body-spin.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > OP OUT [DEX] > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
add_formula: atomic(+1) + mirage(2) --> 3 ADDs (FB.org reading; IFPA canonical 4 ADD via hidden X-Dex per pt1+pt2)
source_description: Basically a reverse miraging mirage. Eg. From a right toe delay- hopping off the left leg- bring the left leg around the bag from out to in to land to support a right inside out mirage and finally the left leg completes the move by landing a left toe delay.
parser_notes: Cross-source ADD divergence: FB.org-3add and FM both show 3-ADD reading; IFPA canonical is 4 ADD per pt1+pt2 hidden X-Dex from a toe ruling. Preserve source_adds=3 here.
equivalent_to: atom-smasher (live DB; canonical 4 ADD per pt1+pt2 X-Dex)
provenance_notes: FB.org 3-ADD reading does not account for IFPA pt1+pt2 hidden X-Dex carry; IFPA canonical = 4 ADD.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] > SAME CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: from a left toe set, quickly circle the bag from out to in with the left foot. End the move by catching the bag on a left foot clipper.
parser_notes: Fairy modifier compound. pt12 Q4 batch open: fairy-as-modifier status unresolved.
unresolved_questions: Fairy modifier ADD weight pending pt12 Q4 batch.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] (plant) > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt12 Q4 batch open. Alternate name "Fairy Same Pickup" preserved.
unresolved_questions: Fairy modifier ADD weight pending pt12 Q4 batch.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] (plant) > SAME IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt12 Q4 batch open.
unresolved_questions: Fairy modifier ADD weight pending pt12 Q4 batch.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL].
parser_notes: pt12 Q4 batch open.
unresolved_questions: Fairy modifier ADD weight pending pt12 Q4 batch.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (front) SPIN [BOD] > OP OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt3+pt7 inspinning is distinct from spinning (opposite spin direction). Alt notation: TOE > (front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL].

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (front) SPIN [BOD] > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt3+pt7 inspinning. Alt notation: TOE > (front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL].

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] > OP IN/OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
source_description: An around-the-world set (pixie) to the opposite leg-over. E.g., from a right toe set, quickly circle the footbag from in to out with your right foot; hop off the left leg passing this leg over the top of the bag and landing a left toe delay.
parser_notes: Pixie modifier on legover base.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE (plant) > (no plant while) SAME IN [BOD] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: Eg. Set from a right toe. Plant the right foot and raise the left foot. Hop off of the right leg bringing it up and over the bag from in to out catching the bag on a left toe delay. **The left leg does not touch the ground after the initial set.
parser_notes: Symposium modifier on pixie base. No-plant-while qualifier per pt7 symposium.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: From a left toe set, perform a quick in to out dexterity with the setting leg. End by catching the bag on a right foot clipper.
parser_notes: Pixie modifier with opposite-side clipper terminator.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: From a left toe set, perform a quick in to out dexterity with the setting leg. End by catching the bag on a left foot clipper.
parser_notes: Pixie modifier with same-side clipper terminator.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] (plant) > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: From a right toe, perform a quick in to out dexterity (pixie) and touch the ground with your right foot. Then, come back around the bag from out to in ending on a left toe delay.
parser_notes: Pixie modifier with same-side illusion variant. Less common.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (NO PLANT WHILE) OP IN [DEX] > OP IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: Pogo is a set per pt2+pt6 (no ADD bonus). Decomposition framing pending.
unresolved_questions: Whether pogo-pickup is a canonical trick or a no-plant variant of double-pickup.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > OP IN [DEX] > OP OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
source_description: Basically a miraging reverse mirage. Eg. Start from a right toe delay. Set the bag up and immediately start to bring your left leg up and over the bag from in to out. Plant your left leg and quickly jump off of your right leg to execute an out to in dexterity. End the move by catching the bag on your left toe.
parser_notes: pt10 ruled Quantum = +1 modifier. Source equates Toe Blizzard to Quantum Illusion.
provenance_notes: FB.org source parenthetical "(Quantum Illusion)" preserved as alternate name.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (back) SPIN [BOD] > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt1+pt2 Spyro = Gyro family standalone body element. Spyro-as-modifier not formally adjudicated. Alt notation: TOE > (back) SPIN [BOD] > OP OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL].
unresolved_questions: Spyro-as-modifier status.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (back) SPIN [BOD] > SAME OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: Alt name "Spyro Legover" preserved. Alt notation: TOE > (back) SPIN [BOD] > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL].
unresolved_questions: Spyro-as-modifier status.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (back) SPIN [BOD] > SAME IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: Alt notation: TOE > (back) SPIN [BOD] > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL].
unresolved_questions: Spyro-as-modifier status.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (back) SPIN [BOD] > SAME IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: Alt name "Spyro Pickup" preserved. Alt notation: TOE > (back) SPIN [BOD] > OP IN [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL].
unresolved_questions: Spyro-as-modifier status.

symbolic_notation_raw: TOE > OP OUT [DEX] (plant) > SAME OUT [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
parser_notes: pt3 tapping = +1 modifier. Distinct from stepping per pt3.

symbolic_notation_raw: CLIP > (NO PLANT WHILE) OP IN [DEX] [BOD] > OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: Pogo straight to butterfly delay. Eg. From a right clipper delay- hop over the footbag with the left without putting the right leg down. Then go straight to a left butterfly.
unresolved_questions: Pogo modifier mechanics + no-plant-while qualifier interaction; canonical decomposition framing.

symbolic_notation_raw: INSIDE > (JUMP) [BOD] > OP SWIRL [DEX] > SAME CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
source_description: A hopping over inside around the world delay. Holding an inside delay close to the ground- the support leg jumps the suspended footbag as the other leg sets the footbag around 4 inches straight up and completes an inside around the world delay which results in ending in a clipper delay position.
